label: Health outcome valuations for US regulatory analysis

qaly.label: Value of a Quality-Adjusted Life Year
qaly.context: Derived from VSL divided by discounted remaining QALYs. Range reflects different derivation methods and VSL base. No single official US QALY value exists.

daly.label: Value of a Disability-Adjusted Life Year averted
daly.context: Treated symmetrically with QALY for CBA purposes

fda_threshold.label: FDA implicit cost-effectiveness threshold
fda_threshold.context: Used in FDA regulatory impact analyses. Lower than the QALY range because it reflects willingness-to-pay for health interventions, not the full VSL-derived QALY.

vsly.label: Value of a Statistical Life Year
vsly.context: Derived from DOT VSL assuming 40-year remaining life expectancy and 2% discount rate. Used less frequently than QALY in US practice.

hhs_standard_values.description: HHS publishes annual 'Standard Values for Regulatory Analysis' with recommended values for common health outcomes.

hospitalization_avoided.note: Average cost of an avoided hospitalization (medical + productivity loss)

emergency_visit_avoided.note: Average cost of an avoided emergency department visit

work_days_lost.note: Value per lost work day (wages + productivity)

source.publication: HHS Standard RIA Values 2025; FDA Regulatory Impact Analysis Guidance
source.publisher: US Department of Health and Human Services; Food and Drug Administration

caveats: No single official US QALY value. The range ($190K-$250K) spans different derivation methods.
QALY values derived from VSL are higher than cost-effectiveness thresholds used by HTA bodies.
The FDA threshold ($150K) is for different purposes than the CBA QALY range.
HHS Standard Values are updated annually and are the most practical source for common health outcomes.
